react.answer:
## Building an Avatar Group in React **Mental model:** An avatar group shows the first N avatars overlapping, then a "+X" bubble for the rest. The logic is pure slicing: `visible = users.slice(0, max)` and `overflow = users.length - max`. Overlap is just negative margin plus a ring so edges read clearly. ### Step 1 — Split visible vs overflow ```jsx const visible = users.slice(0, max); const overflow = Math.max(0, users.length - max); ``` ### Step 2 — Overlap with negative margin Each avatar after the first pulls left; a white ring separates them. ```jsx
{visible.map((u, i) => ( ))}
``` ### Step 3 — Initials fallback If there's no image, render initials on a colour derived from the name. ```jsx const initials = name.split(' ').map((w) => w[0]).slice(0, 2).join('').toUpperCase(); ``` ### Step 4 — The +X bubble Append it only when `overflow > 0`. ```jsx {overflow > 0 && +{overflow}} ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Fewer than max | No "+X" bubble. | | Broken image | Fall back to initials via `onError`. | | Stacking order | Later avatars overlap earlier — set `zIndex` if you want the reverse. | **Interview tip:** Keep the component pure presentation driven by `users` + `max` — deriving `visible`/`overflow` by slicing (not storing them) is the clean, testable approach interviewers favour.

vue.answer:
## Building an Avatar Group in Vue 3 **Mental model:** Show the first N avatars overlapping, then a "+X" bubble for the rest. It's pure slicing derived with `computed`: `visible = users.slice(0, max)` and `overflow = users.length - max`. Overlap is negative margin plus a ring. ### Step 1 — Derive visible + overflow ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Overlap with negative margin ```vue
{{ initials(u.name) }}
``` ### Step 3 — Initials + deterministic colour Derive both from the name so the same person always looks the same. ```js const initials = (name) => name.split(' ').map((w) => w[0]).slice(0, 2).join('').toUpperCase(); ``` ### Step 4 — The +X bubble ```vue
+{{ overflow }}
``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Fewer than max | No "+X" bubble. | | Broken image | Fall back to initials. | | Reactivity | `computed` re-slices when `users`/`max` change. | **Interview tip:** Deriving `visible`/`overflow` with `computed` rather than storing them keeps the component a pure function of its props — that purity is what makes it trivial to test and reason about.
